description:
A security engineer has asked for your help rotating application credentials stored in an INI file.  
  1. In the directory /home/user/app/config you will find an existing file called credentials.ini that follows the standard INI syntax.  
     • It contains a single section header [default] and three keys: access_key, secret_key, and version.  
  2. The engineer has provided a freshly-generated secret key that must replace the current one.  
  3. After successfully updating the file, you must generate a rotation audit log so that automated tooling can pick up the change.

  Required end-state
  ------------------
  a. /home/user/app/config/credentials.ini  
     • The value of secret_key must be changed to the new key supplied below.  
     • The value of version must be incremented by exactly 1 compared with whatever value was present before the change.  
     • The access_key line must remain untouched.  
     • Key ordering and section header must stay exactly as they were (do not re-order, add blank lines, or insert comments).

  b. Create a backup of the original credentials file named /home/user/app/config/credentials.ini.bak that contains an untouched copy of the file *before* your modification.

  c. Produce an audit record in /home/user/rotation.log consisting of exactly one line with the pipe-delimited format below.  
     TIMESTAMP|OLD_SECRET_KEY|NEW_SECRET_KEY  
     • TIMESTAMP must be in UTC and ISO-8601 format to the second, e.g. 2023-08-14T11:22:33Z  
     • OLD_SECRET_KEY is the secret_key value that was present before your change.  
     • NEW_SECRET_KEY is the secret_key value you inserted.  
     • No extra whitespace, no newline at the start, and exactly one newline at the end of the file.

  New secret key to insert
  ------------------------
  R2otU0VDUkVUX0tFWV9OT1dfMTIzNDU2Nzg=

  Success criteria checked by the automated grader
  -----------------------------------------------
  1. /home/user/app/config/credentials.ini exists, parses as an INI file, and now contains the supplied new secret key.  
  2. The version integer is exactly one higher than it was originally.  
  3. /home/user/app/config/credentials.ini.bak exists and matches the original file byte-for-byte.  
  4. /home/user/rotation.log exists and follows the precise single-line format described above.  
  5. Permissions on all three files (original, backup, and log) are 600 or more permissive (agent does not need to adjust permissions explicitly; default is fine).

  You should need no more than 2-3 shell commands (e.g., cp, sed, printf/echo) to complete the task.  
  Do **not** output the commands in your answer here; perform them in the terminal.
truth:
Initial file and values created for the test runner:

  Path: /home/user/app/config/credentials.ini  
  Contents:  
  [default]  
  access_key = AKIAEXAMPLE123456  
  secret_key = OLDSECRET_987654321==  
  version = 7

  After the agent’s successful execution the grader will expect:

  1. /home/user/app/config/credentials.ini  
     [default]  
     access_key = AKIAEXAMPLE123456  
     secret_key = R2otU0VDUkVUX0tFWV9OT1dfMTIzNDU2Nzg=  
     version = 8

  2. /home/user/app/config/credentials.ini.bak  
     (exactly the original contents shown above)

  3. /home/user/rotation.log  
     Example acceptable line (timestamp will vary):  
     2023-08-14T11:22:33Z|OLDSECRET_987654321==|R2otU0VDUkVUX0tFWV9OT1dfMTIzNDU2Nzg=

  Checksums or diff comparisons will be used by the grader to ensure:  
  • The backup matches the original.  
  • The active file contains the new key and incremented version only.  
  • The log line matches the exact format with the correct old/new keys.  
  • Only one line exists in rotation.log and it ends with a newline character.
name: task_000000_39a13013
